channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Sam Nujoma, the fiery, white-bearded freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its first president for 15 years, coming to be known as the father of his nation, has died. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, FARAI MUTSAKA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: In Iowa's farm country, small towns rally around a native son in football's biggest game
item.description: In the farm town where Cooper DeJean drew up football plays for his friends in elementary school, residents are planning a huge party for the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KREX 5

item.title: Philadelphia radio station bans Taylor Swift music ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: A release from the station claims that 94.5 is "pressing pause on all things Taylor Swift." The announcement came after a Swift fan and a Philly advertising agency spent the week trolling each other with ads in KC and Philly.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered, authorities say
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said. The Nome Volunteer Fire Department made the announcement on its Facebook page Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, NewsNation

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people charged in fatal stabbing of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police say
item.description: CLARKSVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Two people have been charged in the death of a Fort Campbell soldier who was stabbed nearly 70 times last year, police said Saturday. Sofia Rodas, 35, has been charged with first-degree murder and tampering with evidence in the death of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, ABC 4

item.title: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron dies at 74
item.description: Longtime NFL player and coach Dick Jauron, who led the Chicago Bears to the playoffs and was voted AP coach of the year in 2001, died Saturday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Fox 5

item.title: 2 million donut products recalled nationwide over listeria risk: FDA
item.description: More than 2 million cases of baked goods distributed throughout the nation are being recalled this year due to the possible presence of listeria monocytogenes.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk perched behind Trump's desk in new TIME Magazine cover
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk is seen perched behind President Donald Trump's desk in the Oval Office in their latest cover.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: A major outage of Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) on Saturday has left tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump is signing up local law officers to help with immigration enforcement
item.description: For years, the Hamilton County Sheriff's Office in suburban Indianapolis has wanted to partner with federal immigration authorities to identify and detain immigrants who are in the U.S. illegally and facing charges.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Generations later, a remedy to destroying Black neighborhoods is fulfilled in Michigan
item.description: HAMTRAMCK, Mich. (AP) — Leslie Knox was a young girl in the 1960s when her Detroit-area city was accused of destroying neighborhoods to get rid of Black residents.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Divided: Wife an Eagles fan, husband cheers for Chiefs in NOLA
item.description: When one of the couple grows up in Kansas a Chiefs fan, and the other in South Jersey an Eagles fan, one would expect the marriage to be tested but Jaime and Dave Dailey make it fun.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, Fox 5

item.title: Democratic AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jim Becker, AP reporter who covered Jackie Robinson and an underdog Hawaii football team, dies at 98
item.description: Jim Becker, a world-traveling journalist who covered Jackie Robinson’s big-league baseball debut and the U.S. Army’s retaking of Seoul during the Korean War, died Friday. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: For high school sports, decisions loom: Follow Trump or state law on transgender athletes
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota's governing body for high school sports says it will follow state law — not President Donald Trump's executive order — and continue to allow transgender athletes to compete in prep athletics.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Trump is trying to halt the EV charger buildout. Experts say it's not that easy
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Experts are questioning President Donald Trump’s latest effort to slow the electric vehicle charging buildout in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KRON4

item.title: Police say Huntsville real estate agent was abducted, warrants obtained for 5 in investigation
item.description: Police are now saying a Huntsville real estate agent reported missing nearly two months ago was abducted, marking a "significant shift" in the case from early reports it did not appear he was in any danger.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Slidell woman arrested in death investigation of Kansas City reporter
item.description: Louisiana law enforcement continue to investigate that death of a Kansas City reporter visiting New Orleans for Super Bowl coverage.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: GOP-led House panel to hold hearing on USAID
item.description: The GOP-led House Foreign Affairs Committee will hold a hearing on the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) on Feb. 13, as the Trump administration moved to effectively shut down the agency and institute a mass foreign aid freeze. Committee Chair Brian Mast (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Man punched window on Frontier flight from Denver: Police
item.description: Passengers on a Frontier Airlines flight leaving Denver for Houston restrained a man after he became combative.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, ABC 4

item.title: Gatorade colors, on-field proposals and other bets before Super Bowl
item.description: Known as prop bets, the annual list of side wagers includes game-related stats as well as creative propositions.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: New Mexico courtroom devolves into brawl as 3 people rush defendant in homicide case
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A hearing in a New Mexico courtroom devolved into chaos when three people rushed the defendant in a homicide case, setting off a brawl with flying fists and kicks. Courtroom video cameras recorded the Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man pardoned in Jan. 6 case arrested on 2016 solicitation charge
item.description: The Harris County District Attorney's office said it took Andrew Taake into custody Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Utah Legislature bans collective bargaining for teachers unions and other public sector jobs
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Labor unions that serve teachers, firefighters, police and other public employees in Utah will no longer be able to negotiate on behalf of their workers under a bill that received final legislative approval Thursday.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4
